messages.content: rewrite "TTM models can be broken down into three main parts: text encoder, generative model and decoder. The text encoder maps the input text into an embedding representation." for a paper
The TTM models consist of three primary components: the text encoder, generative model, and decoder. The text encoder is responsible for translating the input text into an embedding representation.

rewrite "Different text encoders are employed in the literature. MusicLM \cite{musicLM} uses MuLan \cite{mulan}, a contrastive language-audio pretrained model that links the text and audio embedding pairs. This dual encoder allows to train on only music data using the audio encoder and run inference with text through the text encoder In MusicGen \cite{musicGen} different text encoders are explored T5 \cite{raffel2020exploring}, FLAN-T5 \cite{flanT5}, and CLAP \cite{laionclap}, showing that T5 provides the best performance. The T5 and FLAN-T5 are language models so the training and inference is done with text only as input, whereas CLAP is a contrastive language-audio pretrained model similar to MuLan therefore audio can be used as input during training. Other works use CLAP such as \cite{stableAudio,audioldm,audioldm2} and T5 \cite{magnet} or Flan-T5 \cite{mustango}. In \cite{audioldm2} other encoders are included to incorporate other modalities." for a paper

In existing literature, various text encoders have been utilized. For instance, MusicLM (musicLM) leverages MuLan (mulan), a dual encoder model that integrates text and audio embedding pairs through contrastive pretraining. This allows for training solely on music data using the audio encoder, while facilitating inference with text through the text encoder. In MusicGen (musicGen), different text encoders such as T5 (raffel2020exploring), FLAN-T5 (flanT5), and CLAP (laionclap) are examined, with results showing that T5 delivers the most optimal performance. T5 and FLAN-T5 function as language models, requiring text input for both training and inference, whereas CLAP is a contrastive language-audio pretrained model akin to MuLan, thus enabling the use of audio input during training. Other studies like the ones referenced in (stableAudio,audioldm,audioldm2,magnet,mustango) also incorporate CLAP, T5, and Flan-T5 as text encoders, with (audioldm2) exploring additional encoders to incorporate other modalities.

rewrite "The advancements in generative AI enables a wide range of new applications such as text-to-music (TTM) systems where users can create music content via text prompt. [motivation] Recent works on this topic, such as MusicGen \cite{musicGen} or MusicLDM \cite{musicldm}, can generate high quality music samples showing the potential of TTM." for a paper

Recent advancements in generative AI have paved the way for a plethora of new applications, including text-to-music (TTM) systems that empower users to craft music content through text prompts. This burgeoning field of research, exemplified by projects like MusicGen (musicGen) and MusicLDM (musicldm), has demonstrated the capability of generating high-quality music samples, underscoring the vast potential of TTM technology.

rewrite "Recent advancements in generative AI have enable a wide range of new applications such as text-to-music (TTM) systems where users can create music content via text prompts. This allows users to quickly create music pieces that satisfies their needs without requiring to be expert musicians." for technical paper

Recent developments in generative AI have facilitated the emergence of various novel applications, including text-to-music (TTM) systems that empower users to generate music content through textual prompts. This capability enables users to efficiently craft music compositions that cater to their specific requirements, eliminating the necessity for expertise in music composition.

rewrite "Recent developments in generative AI have facilitated the emergence of various novel applications, including text-to-music (TTM) systems that empower users to generate music content through textual prompts." for technical paper

Recent advancements in generative AI have led to the emergence of a range of innovative applications, such as text-to-music (TTM) systems, which empower users to create music content using textual prompts.
rewrite "underscoring the vast potential of TTM technology."
highlighting the substantial potential of TTM technology.
rewrite “underscoring the vast potential of TTM technology.”
emphasizing the significant potential of TTM technology.
write "Reducing the computational complexity of TTM models is key to allow running these systems on source constrained devices." for paper
Minimizing the computational complexity of TTM models is essential to enable the deployment of these systems on resource-constrained devices.
rewrite "These sizes are prohibit to deploy" for paper
These sizes are impractical for deployment.

rewrite "However, minimizing the size of TTM models is essential to enable the deployment of these systems on resource-constrained devices. For instance in \cite{musicGen} the total number of parameters of entire TTM is 588M parameters, also in in \cite{audioldm} is 428M parameters. These sizes are impractical for deployment in the aforementioned devices. Therefore we propose in this work to reduce the TTM size below 100M parameters." for technical paper

Nonetheless, it is crucial to reduce the size of TTM models for their deployment on resource-constrained devices. For example, in \cite{musicGen}, the combined total number of parameters in the TTM model is 588 million, and in \cite{audioldm} it is 428 million. Such large sizes make deployment on said devices unfeasible. Hence, our proposed approach in this study aims to decrease the TTM model size to under 100 million parameters.

rewrite "As shown in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}, we propose to take the MusicGen-Small pretrained model and reduce its initial size of 588M parameters down to \red{89M??} parameters. The approach proposed reduces each of the three components of the TTM individually through a combination of distillation and fine-tuning using MusicBench \cite{mustango}." for technical paper

Illustrated in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}, our approach involves taking the MusicGen-Small pretrained model and decreasing its original size of 588 million parameters to \red{89 million??} parameters. The proposed method entails reducing the size of each of the three components of the TTM model individually through a blend of distillation and fine-tuning techniques, leveraging MusicBench \cite{mustango}.

rewrite “As shown in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}, we propose to take the MusicGen-Small pretrained model and reduce its initial size of 588M parameters down to \red{89M??} parameters. The approach proposed reduces each of the three components of the TTM individually through a combination of distillation and fine-tuning using MusicBench \cite{mustango}.” for technical paper

Illustrated in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}, our strategy involves utilizing the MusicGen-Small pretrained model and decreasing its original parameter count from 588 million to \red{89 million??}. This reduction process is achieved by individually optimizing each of the three components of the TTM model through a method that combines distillation and fine-tuning techniques with the aid of MusicBench \cite{mustango}.
write "Contributions of this work" for technical paper
"Contributions of this study"
rewrite "Creation of a tiny TTM under 100M parameters which shows comparable performance with larger systems and can be targeted to resource-constrained devices."  for technical paper
Development of a compact TTM model with fewer than 100 million parameters, demonstrating performance on par with larger systems and suitable for deployment on resource-limited devices.
Development of a compact TTM model having less than 100 million parameters, exhibiting performance comparable to larger systems, and optimized for deployment on resource-constrained devices.
rewrite “Creation of a tiny TTM under 100M parameters which shows comparable performance with larger systems and can be targeted to resource-constrained devices.” for technical paper

Development of a compact TTM model containing fewer than 100 million parameters, demonstrating performance comparable to larger systems, and specifically designed for deployment on resource-constrained devices.
synonims for "Creation

2. Formation

4. Production

6. Origination

10. Construction

rewrite "The creation of a tiny TTM system containing fewer than 100M parameters, for first time as far as the authors are aware, demonstrating performance comparable to larger systems and more suitable for deployment on resource-constrained devices." for technical paper

The development of a compact TTM system with less than 100M parameters, believed to be a novel achievement by the authors, showcasing performance on par with larger systems and optimized for deployment on resource-constrained devices.

rewrite "A Knowledge distillation method based on randomly weighting the terms in the distillation loss for each step. Two different distributions are explored to withdraw the random variable." for technical paper

An approach to Knowledge distillation is proposed, involving the random weighting of terms in the distillation loss at each step. Two distinct distributions are investigated for selecting the random variable.
